Title: Gate-Level 4-Bit Arithmetic (Rosetta-style netlist)
Statement: The four elementary operations on 4-bit unsigned naturals, each computed by a logic-gate netlist and witnessed to agree with the Peano results for the four flagship cases.
Scope: 4-bit unsigned add (ripple-carry), truncated/two's-complement subtract, shift-and-add multiply (8-bit product), and restoring divide. Nothing arithmetic is stored: only input bits are data; every downstream bit is a fixed-point computation over gate truth tables and the netlist. Width is a data parameter -- the same engine settles 2, 4, 8, or N bits unchanged.
TrustBoundary: LogicGateKernel: the AND/OR/XOR/NOT truth tables (14 rows) and the netlist connection semantics are the trusted base. No theorem is imported above the gate kernel.
ComponentHierarchy: sub4 = adder4 + NOT on B + carry-in 1 (two's complement)
mul4 = 4 x shift-and-add rows of adder4 -> 8-bit product
div4 = restoring division: 4 stages of sub5 + per-bit mux2

Conclusions.Statement: sub4(4,2) settles to output bits 0010 = 2, borrow 0
div4(4,2) settles to quotient bits 0010 = 2, remainder 0

SuppliesToConsumer.Note: The Peano Successor derivations already establish the four flagship facts internally. This provider re-establishes the identical four facts on a disjoint substrate (logic gates), so the network holds two independent zero-import witnesses for 2+2=4, 4-2=2, 2*2=4, 4/2=2. This is a corroborating witness, not a load-bearing import.

SubstrateEquivalence.ExhaustiveNetlistValidation: adder4/sub4/mul4 verified on all 256 4-bit input pairs; div4 on all 240 non-zero-divisor pairs, against reference arithmetic in the validator harness only.
